metadata.title: Debug Training Pipeline — Quasi-E2E Mechanics Validation
metadata.estimated-duration: 90–180 min (depends on number of bugs found)

dual-mandate.mandate: MECHANICS VALIDATION: Verify that the Docker → Prefect → MLflow → train → post_training → analyze chain runs without crashing for every model listed in configs/experiment/debug_all_models.yaml. Numbers do not matter. Any run that completes without exception = PASS.

BUG FIXING PROTOCOL (non-negotiable): 1. Observe failure → read FULL traceback (not just last line) 2. Identify root cause (wrong import, missing env var, bad config key, etc.) 3. Create GitHub issue: gh issue create --title "fix(train-debug): [root cause]" 4. Write failing unit test that reproduces the root cause 5. Fix the root cause (not the symptom) 6. Verify fix: re-run only the failing step, not the full suite 7. Commit fix to test/debug-training-pipeline branch BANNED: adding try/except to silence errors, hardcoded fallback values, skipping the failing test, re-running hoping it goes away.

phase.name: Pre-run blockers — fix before anything else
phase.rationale: Reviewer agents identified 3 blockers that will crash the debug pipeline before any training runs. These must be fixed first using TDD (red→green).

task.name: RED: Tests for post_training UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT propagation
task.description:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_post_training_upstream_experiment.py:
class TestPostTrainingUpstreamExperiment: - test_finds_upstream_from_env_var(monkeypatch) — set UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=foo, mock find_upstream_safely, verify it is called with experiment_name="foo" - test_hardcoded_experiment_name_banned() — parse post_training_flow.py with ast.parse(); verify no Constant node with value "minivess_training" exists in the flow body (using ast.walk(), NOT regex — Rule #16) - test_flow_logs_to_upstream_experiment(monkeypatch) — set EXPERIMENT=debug_foo, mock mlflow.set_experiment, verify called with "debug_foo" - test_main_passes_upstream_to_flow(monkeypatch) — set UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=bar, mock post_training_flow(), verify call receives the env var value

task.name: GREEN: Fix post_training_flow hardcoded experiment names
task.description:
Fix src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py:
1. In find_upstream_safely call (currently line 270): upstream_exp = os.environ.get("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT", "minivess_training") upstream = find_upstream_safely( tracking_uri=tracking_uri, experiment_name=upstream_exp, upstream_flow="train", )
2. In mlflow.set_experiment (currently line 279): mlflow_exp = os.environ.get("EXPERIMENT", "minivess_training") mlflow.set_experiment(mlflow_exp)
3. Fix __main__ block to pass env var to flow: _upstream_experiment = _os.environ.get("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT") post_training_flow() # ensure flow reads env var internally (step 1 above handles it)

task.name: RED: Tests for analysis_flow Docker entry point
task.description:
Create tests/unit/orchestration/test_analysis_flow_entry_point.py:
class TestAnalysisFlowEntryPoint: - test_entry_point_from_env_exists() — import analysis_flow; assert hasattr module, "_entry_point_from_env" - test_entry_point_reads_upstream_experiment(monkeypatch, tmp_path) — set UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=foo, mock find_upstream_safely to return a fake run, verify _entry_point_from_env() does not raise and returns non-None params - test_main_block_does_not_raise_on_upstream_experiment(monkeypatch) — set UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=foo, mock _entry_point_from_env, verify __main__ no longer raises SystemExit unconditionally - test_main_raises_without_upstream_experiment() — clear UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT, verify __main__ raises RuntimeError (not SystemExit) with useful message

task.name: GREEN: Implement analysis_flow Docker entry point
task.description:
Implement in src/minivess/orchestration/flows/analysis_flow.py:
def _entry_point_from_env() -> dict[str, Any]: """Discover analysis parameters from UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT env var.""" upstream_exp = os.environ.get("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT") if not upstream_exp: raise RuntimeError( "UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT not set. " "Run: docker compose run -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=debug_full_pipeline analyze" ) tracking_uri = resolve_tracking_uri() upstream = find_upstream_safely( tracking_uri=tracking_uri, experiment_name=upstream_exp, upstream_flow="train", ) if not upstream: raise RuntimeError(f"No training runs found in experiment '{upstream_exp}'") # Load config from MLflow artifact (or use minimal defaults for debug) config_dict = _load_config_from_mlflow(upstream["run_id"], tracking_uri) eval_config = _build_eval_config_from_dict(config_dict) model_config_dict = _build_model_config_from_dict(config_dict) dataloaders_dict = _build_dataloaders_from_config(config_dict) return { "eval_config": eval_config, "model_config_dict": model_config_dict, "dataloaders_dict": dataloaders_dict, "upstream_training_run_id": upstream["run_id"], "tracking_uri": tracking_uri, }
Replace __main__ block: if __name__ == "__main__": _require_docker_context() params = _entry_point_from_env() result = run_analysis_flow(**params) sys.exit(0 if result.get("status") == "success" else 1)
Note: This is a significant refactor. The helper functions (_load_config_from_mlflow, _build_eval_config_from_dict, etc.) must be implemented using MLflow artifacts from the training run. If these artifacts don't exist (debug runs), fall back to minimal defaults (debug=True mode).

task.name: RED+GREEN: Create 4 missing YAML configs
task.description:
1. RED: Add to tests/unit/config/test_debug_experiment_configs.py: class TestModelConfigs: - test_all_debug_models_have_model_yaml() — for each model in debug_all_models.yaml[models_to_test], assert Path(f"configs/model/{model}.yaml").exists() - test_all_debug_models_have_profile_yaml() — for each model, assert Path(f"configs/model_profiles/{model}.yaml").exists() or model == "dynunet" (dynunet profile YAML is dynunet.yaml, matches)
2. GREEN: Create: configs/model/comma_mamba.yaml — references CommaAdapter configs/model/ulike_mamba.yaml — references MambaAdapter configs/model_profiles/sam3_vanilla.yaml — overhead=400MB, bytes_per_voxel_amp=12, gpu_low batch=1, patch_xy=64, notes="SDPA mandatory; attn_implementation=sdpa enforced" configs/model_profiles/sam3_hybrid.yaml — similar to vanilla + DynUNet fusion overhead

task.name: Fix run_debug.sh: pass EXPERIMENT to post_training and analyze
task.description:
In scripts/run_debug.sh, update the flow chaining section (lines 225-228):
BEFORE: docker compose -f "$FLOWS_COMPOSE" run --rm \ -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT="$EXPERIMENT" \ "$flow" 2>&1 | tee "$FLOW_LOG"
AFTER: docker compose -f "$FLOWS_COMPOSE" run --rm \ -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT="$EXPERIMENT" \ -e EXPERIMENT="$EXPERIMENT" \ "$flow" 2>&1 | tee "$FLOW_LOG"
This propagates both: UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT: where to find training runs (read-only) EXPERIMENT: where to log own results (write)

task.name: Verify all pre-run fixes pass tests
task.steps: uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_post_training_upstream_experiment.py -v uv run pytest tests/unit/orchestration/test_analysis_flow_entry_point.py -v uv run pytest tests/unit/config/test_debug_experiment_configs.py -v uv run pre-commit run --all-files

phase.name: Infrastructure preflight
phase.rationale: Catch obvious blockers before any training runs. Building Docker images up-front avoids mid-run surprises.

task.name: Verify dev services are running
task.expected: PostgreSQL, MinIO, MLflow, Prefect all in "Up" or "running" state. If not: just dev
task.on-failure: Services not starting = infrastructure bug. Check docker-compose.yml, port conflicts (mlflow:5000, prefect:4200, postgres:5432, minio:9000). Fix the compose file, do NOT use host-env fallbacks.

task.name: Verify and build required Docker images
task.steps:
# Check which images already exist: docker images --format "table {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" | grep minivess
# Known to exist: minivess-base:latest, minivess-train:latest (20.6 GB each) # Likely MISSING: minivess-post-training, minivess-analyze # Build only what is missing (do NOT rebuild train — 20 GB, takes 30+ min): docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml build post_training analyze
task.expected: minivess-post-training:latest and minivess-analyze:latest exist after build.
task.on-failure: Build error → read Dockerfile.post_training / Dockerfile.analyze. Common causes: missing apt package, bad COPY path, Python import at build time. Fix the Dockerfile. Open GitHub issue.

task.name: Dry-run preflight check
task.expected: Script prints DRY-RUN commands for: train (dynunet) + post_training + analyze. No errors. Confirms: experiment YAML exists, Docker daemon available, minivess-base:latest found.

task.name: Verify all models_to_test have config files
task.steps: for model in dynunet sam3_vanilla sam3_hybrid comma_mamba ulike_mamba; do if [ ! -f "configs/model/${model}.yaml" ]; then echo "MISSING: configs/model/${model}.yaml — run Phase -1 task T-1.5" exit 1 fi echo " ✓ configs/model/${model}.yaml" done
task.expected: All 5 model config YAMLs found (created in Phase -1 T-1.5).

task.name: Check Docker named volumes exist
task.expected: Volumes: minivess_raw_data, minivess_data_cache, minivess_configs_splits, minivess_checkpoint_cache, minivess_mlruns_data, minivess_logs_data, minivess_post_training_out, minivess_outputs_analysis exist. If not: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm train true (creates volumes on first run)

phase.name: Stage 1 — DynUNet full chain (debug_full_pipeline)
phase.rationale: Validate the train→post_training→analyze chain with the most reliable model (DynUNet, 3.5 GB VRAM) before testing all 5 models. Fix chain-level bugs here.
phase.config-summary: model=dynunet, losses=[cbdice_cldice], max_epochs=2, max_train_volumes=2, max_val_volumes=2, num_folds=3, flows=[train, post_training, analyze], post_training.plugins=[swa, calibration]

task.name: Run debug_full_pipeline
task.steps: just debug-pipeline # OR: bash scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_full_pipeline # Logs: outputs/debug/logs/{TIMESTAMP}_dynunet.log # outputs/debug/logs/{TIMESTAMP}_post_training.log # outputs/debug/logs/{TIMESTAMP}_analyze.log # Summary: outputs/debug/summary_{TIMESTAMP}.json
task.expected-per-flow: train/dynunet: Prefect flow completes, 6 runs logged to MLflow (3 folds × 1 loss). post_training: SWA checkpoint + calibration artifacts written to /app/outputs/post_training. analyze: Comparison table artifact written to MLflow. Summary JSON: all statuses = "OK".

task.name: Inspect MLflow runs after Stage 1
task.steps: # From the host (not in Docker): uv run mlflow ui --backend-store-uri mlruns --port 5001 & # Or query directly: uv run python3 -c " import mlflow mlflow.set_tracking_uri('mlruns') runs = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_names=['debug_full_pipeline']) print(runs[['run_id','status','tags.mlflow.runName']].head(20)) "
task.expected: 6 runs with status=FINISHED. Each run has: train_loss, val_dsc, val_cldice metrics. config/resolved_config.yaml artifact present (validates Rule #23 Hydra logging).
task.failure-modes-to-investigate: - status=FAILED → read mlflow run artifacts for error_type tag + traceback - Missing config artifact → compose_experiment_config() or log_hydra_config() not called - 0 runs → MLflow tracking URI mismatch (check MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in container vs host)

task.name: Verify post_training MLflow contract
task.steps: # post_training_flow uses find_upstream_safely() to locate training runs. # Confirm it found the debug_full_pipeline runs: grep -i "upstream" outputs/debug/logs/*post_training*.log | tail -20 # OR inside the container: docker compose -f deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml run --rm \ -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=debug_full_pipeline \ -e MINIVESS_ALLOW_HOST=0 \ post_training
task.expected: Log shows: "Found N upstream runs for experiment=debug_full_pipeline" SWA checkpoint: /app/outputs/post_training/swa_*.pt Calibration: /app/outputs/post_training/calibration_*.json

task.name: Verify analysis flow MLflow contract
task.expected: Log shows: ComparisonTable created with N=1 loss × 1 model. Champion tagged in MLflow. Analysis output: /app/outputs/analysis/comparison_table.md

phase.name: Bug triage protocol — after Stage 1
phase.rationale: Stage 1 will almost certainly surface bugs. This phase defines HOW to handle them systematically before moving to all-model testing. No panic fixing.
phase.bug-triage-protocol:
For EACH failure observed in Phase 1:
1. CLASSIFY: Which category? - ImportError / ModuleNotFoundError → missing dependency or wrong package name - AttributeError / KeyError → API mismatch between flow and expected config key - RuntimeError from _require_docker_context() → flow run outside Docker (not expected here) - MLflow MlflowException → tracking URI mismatch, experiment not found - CUDA OOM → patch size / batch too large for 8 GB (check model profile) - FileNotFoundError → volume not mounted, wrong path in compose
2. SEARCH: Before writing any fix, search the codebase: - grep the exact error class/message in src/ to understand what raises it - Check the relevant CLAUDE.md for the module (Layer 1 context) - Check orchestration/CLAUDE.md for inter-flow contract rules
3. CREATE ISSUE: gh issue create \ --title "fix(debug-pipeline): [short root cause]" \ --label "bug,test-infra" \ --body "[Full traceback + affected file:line + root cause analysis]"
4. WRITE TEST: # Unit test that fails with the same error: tests/unit/orchestration/test_[flow_name]_[symptom].py
5. FIX root cause (not symptom).
6. VERIFY: re-run only the failing step: just debug-pipeline # if chain-level just debug # if training only
7. COMMIT: git add + git commit on test/debug-training-pipeline

known-risk-areas.risk: run_debug.sh passes -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT to post_training/analyze containers. But post_training_flow.py uses find_upstream_safely() with experiment_name= os.environ.get("UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT"). Verify this env var flows correctly. Test: docker compose run --rm -e UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT=debug_full_pipeline post_training

risk.area: MLflow tracking URI inside container vs host

known-risk-areas.risk: MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI in compose = http://minivess-mlflow:5000 (Docker network). On host: mlruns/ directory. These are DIFFERENT backends. Risk: post_training/analyze look for runs that were logged to the Docker MLflow server, but if train_flow writes to the volume-mounted mlruns dir, there's a mismatch. Verify: both train and post_training use the same MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI.

SWA requires checkpoints from training. If debug run uses lightweight checkpoint (configs/checkpoint/lightweight.yaml), SWA may fail to find what it needs. Fix: ensure SWA plugin has a graceful skip (not crash) for missing checkpoints.
ComparisonTable is designed for multi-loss, multi-model experiments. May fail or produce empty comparison for debug_full_pipeline (1 loss, 1 model). Fix: guard against single-row comparison tables.

PR #513 bridged Hydra-zen to train_flow. post_training and analyze may not yet call compose_experiment_config() — they may be reading env vars directly. This is acceptable for now but document as a known gap.

phase.name: Stage 2 — All models, training only (debug_all_models)
phase.rationale: After the chain is verified for DynUNet, test that all 5 models can at least complete 2 epochs × 3 folds × 2 train vols without crashing. Model-specific bugs are isolated here before adding post-training complexity.

task.name: Run debug_all_models
task.steps: just debug-all-models # OR: bash scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models # This runs 5 × docker compose run --rm -e EXPERIMENT=debug_all_models # -e HYDRA_OVERRIDES=model=X train # Logs: outputs/debug/logs/{TIMESTAMP}_{model}.log for each model
task.expected: Each model completes 6 runs (3 folds × 1 loss). Summary JSON shows all OK. DynUNet should be the most reliable. SAM3 models may fail on missing HF weights.

task.name: Model-specific expected failure modes
task.model: *** REVIEWER B CORRECTION: risk downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM *** Adapter: src/minivess/adapters/sam3_vanilla.py — COMPLETE. VRAM: 2.9 GB measured (SDPA mandatory, hardcoded in sam3_backbone.py:174-190). SDPA is enforced in code — eager mode (7+ GB OOM) cannot happen accidentally. Requires HF_TOKEN in .env (HuggingFace gated model). This is the only real risk. Action: ensure HF_TOKEN is set. If gated download fails → skip and create issue. Profile YAML: created in Phase -1 T-1.5.

*** REVIEWER B CORRECTION: risk downgraded from HIGH to MEDIUM *** Adapter: src/minivess/adapters/sam3_hybrid.py — COMPLETE. VRAM: ~3.4 GB (sam3_vanilla + DynUNet fusion). Fits 8 GB with SDPA. Same HF_TOKEN requirement as vanilla. Profile YAML: created in Phase -1 T-1.5.

*** REVIEWER B CORRECTION: adapter is COMPLETE (src/minivess/adapters/comma.py) *** VRAM: ~2.5 GB, batch=1 (gpu_low), patch=(48,48,24) required. Risk: memory scales with volume — ensure patch size constraint from profile. Missing: configs/model/comma_mamba.yaml (created in Phase -1 T-1.5).

*** REVIEWER B CONFIRMS: adapter is COMPLETE (src/minivess/adapters/mamba.py) *** Pure-PyTorch SSM approximation (no mamba-ssm CUDA kernels). batch=4 on 8 GB. Missing: configs/model/ulike_mamba.yaml (created in Phase -1 T-1.5).
Already verified in Stage 1. Should pass cleanly.

task.name: After each model run — check VRAM
task.steps: # Between models, check GPU VRAM is recovered: nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used --format=csv,noheader,nounits
task.expected: VRAM returns to ~0 MB between models (Docker container stops, GPU memory freed). If VRAM leaks: docker stop force + docker system prune -f (and investigate).

phase.name: Bug triage — model-specific failures
phase.protocol:
Same triage protocol as Phase 2. For model-specific failures: - If model adapter is missing → create GitHub issue, exclude model from debug_all_models by adding it to a skip_models list in the YAML (new config key). - If VRAM OOM → check model profile (configs/model_profiles/{model}.yaml) for gpu_low batch size. Verify HYDRA_OVERRIDES are passing the right batch size. - If import error for a model → check src/minivess/adapters/{model}_adapter.py exists.
Rule: NEVER add a try/except around model init to skip failing models silently. Either fix it properly or exclude it explicitly via config.

phase.name: Stage 3 — All models + full chain (new combined config)
phase.rationale: After Stage 1 (chain works for DynUNet) and Stage 2 (all models train), combine them: all WORKING models (skip failed ones from Phase 4) + full chain. This requires a new experiment config.

task.name: Create debug_all_models_pipeline.yaml
task.description:
Create configs/experiment/debug_all_models_pipeline.yaml:
@package _global_ # All working 8GB models + full train→post_training→analyze chain. # Models: set to those that passed Phase 3 (remove failed ones). # Duration: ~60 min. defaults: - override /checkpoint: lightweight
experiment_name: debug_all_models_pipeline losses: - cbdice_cldice max_epochs: 2 debug: true max_train_volumes: 2 max_val_volumes: 2 num_folds: 3
flows: - train - post_training - analyze
post_training: plugins: - swa - calibration
models_to_test: - dynunet # add other working models from Phase 3
Write failing test FIRST (TDD): tests/unit/config/test_debug_experiment_configs.py: class TestDebugAllModelsPipeline: - test_composes_without_error() - test_has_models_to_test() - test_has_flows_including_post_training() - test_has_flows_including_analyze()

task.name: Run debug_all_models_pipeline
task.steps: bash scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models_pipeline # OR after adding justfile recipe: # just debug-all-pipeline
task.expected: All working models complete train → post_training → analyze. Summary JSON: all flows OK. MLflow: N_models × 6 runs (3 folds × 1 loss) all FINISHED.

task.name: Add debug-all-pipeline recipe to justfile
task.description: Add to justfile: # All working models + full chain (train → post_training → analyze) debug-all-pipeline: bash scripts/run_debug.sh --experiment debug_all_models_pipeline

phase.name: Observability: monitoring the debug run in real-time
phase.rationale: The debug run takes 60-90 min. Use these commands to monitor progress without re-running the whole suite.

task.name: Real-time log monitoring
task.steps:
# Terminal 1: tail the per-model log (run_debug.sh creates these): tail -f outputs/debug/logs/$(ls -t outputs/debug/logs/ | head -1)
# Terminal 2: MLflow run status: watch -n 10 "uv run python3 -c \" import mlflow; mlflow.set_tracking_uri('mlruns') r = mlflow.search_runs(experiment_names=['debug_all_models_pipeline']) print(r[['tags.mlflow.runName','status','metrics.val_dsc']].tail(10)) \""
# Terminal 3: GPU utilization: watch -n 5 nvidia-smi

task.name: Inspect summary after run
task.steps: # Read JSON summary: uv run python3 -c " import json; from pathlib import Path f = sorted(Path('outputs/debug').glob('summary_*.json'))[-1] s = json.loads(f.read_text(encoding='utf-8')) print(json.dumps(s, indent=2)) "
task.expected: All flows have status=OK. Total duration within expected bounds. Any FAILED flow → trigger Phase 2/4 bug triage protocol.

phase.name: Test coverage — prevent regression
phase.rationale: Every bug found in Phases 2/4 gets a regression test. This is the "pipeline improvement" part of the dual mandate.

task.name: Unit tests for debug config
task.expected: All tests pass including new TestDebugAllModelsPipeline class.

task.name: Unit tests for flow integration
task.description: For each bug fixed in Phase 2/4, a corresponding unit test must exist. Naming: tests/unit/orchestration/test_[flow_name]_[failure_symptom].py Examples: test_post_training_upstream_experiment_env_var.py test_analysis_single_model_comparison_table.py test_train_flow_hydra_config_artifact.py

task.name: Run full unit test suite
task.steps: uv run pytest tests/unit/ -x -q uv run ruff check src/ tests/ uv run pre-commit run --all-files

task.name: Commit and push
task.steps: git add configs/experiment/debug_all_models_pipeline.yaml git add justfile git add tests/unit/config/test_debug_experiment_configs.py git add tests/unit/orchestration/ # regression tests from bug triage git add outputs/debug/summary_*.json # for review evidence git commit -m "test(debug-pipeline): quasi-E2E mechanics validation — all models + full chain" git push -u origin test/debug-training-pipeline

task.name: Create PR
task.steps:
gh pr create \ --title "test(debug-pipeline): quasi-E2E debug training validation + bug fixes" \ --body "## Summary - Adds debug_all_models_pipeline.yaml: all working 8GB models + train→post_training→analyze chain - Fixes [list of bugs discovered during run] — each with root cause analysis + regression test - Documents model-specific exclusions (SAM3 missing HF weights, etc.) as GitHub issues - Adds just debug-all-pipeline recipe
## Bugs Fixed [To be filled during run — each bug = 1 GH issue linked here]
## Known Gaps (issues created, not fixed in this PR) [To be filled]
## Validation - Summary JSON at outputs/debug/summary_{TIMESTAMP}.json shows all OK - uv run pytest tests/unit/ passes - pre-commit passes"

reviewer-instructions.reviewer: Verify that the Docker compose services, volume mounts, and env var flow (UPSTREAM_EXPERIMENT, MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI) are correctly described. Check: does post_training actually receive MLflow runs from train? Check: does analyze actually receive post_training artifacts? Flag any steps that would fail given the current state of docker-compose.flows.yml.

Review the model-specific risk assessments in Phase 3. Verify: which models have complete adapter implementations vs stubs. Verify: SAM3 VRAM requirements vs 8GB RTX 2070 Super (see MEMORY.md SAM3 section). Check: comma_mamba and ulike_mamba adapter status in src/minivess/adapters/. Flag any model listed in debug_all_models.yaml that cannot realistically run.
